name: Mexican Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 4 large Red or green sweet peppers
1 1/2 cups Cooked, drained red kidney beans
1 1/2 cups Tomatoes, canned with their juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the peppers and cut them in half lengthwise. Remove the seeds.
2. Place the peppers, cut side facing down, on a rack above boiling water.
3. Cover the pot and steam for about 10 minutes, or until the peppers are just beginning to get soft. Do not stack the peppers; keep them in a single layer.
4. If using a wok with a steaming rack, all peppers can be steamed at once. Otherwise, steam in two batches.
5. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add onions, celery, cumin, chili powder, basil, and oregano. Sauté until onions are almost tender.
6. Add cooked rice and beans. Mix well; then add shoyu or tamari and cayenne to taste. Mix again.
7. Lightly oil a shallow 7 x 11-inch baking dish. Fill each pepper with the bean and rice mixture and place peppers side by side in the dish.
8. Mix canned tomatoes and tomato paste together.
9. If using whole tomatoes, chop or break them with a fork.
10. Pour the tomato mixture over the stuffed peppers.
11. Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
12. Serve with fresh salsa and slices of ripe avocado.
